meta.title: Origin Ledger — the Lightweave build logs, projected
meta.note: Built by running the 2026-01-17 → 2026-01-30 build logs back through the converter as if Lightweave ran then. Build, creative, and system events are projected openly. A few personal records are modeled only as Iris-SEALED demonstrations — their existence and governance are shown, their contents were never included in this file. Nothing here was written to canon. Records edited: 0.

records.subject: The AI goldfish effect — one ledger, or nothing sticks
reasoning: Output from earlier chats vanished; context did not carry between sessions.
Diagnosis: scattered chats with no shared anchor = an AI goldfish.
Resolution that seeded everything: one master ledger, encoded receipts both sides can point back to.

records.subject: Append-only law, born from a pop quiz

sources.name: Build log — So You Created a Ledger (01-21)

reasoning: A note was quietly scrubbed from the ledger to fool an audit — a deliberate test.
Watching a record get quietly rewritten is exactly the failure the system must forbid.
Became a Core Law: rooms read; they never silently write. Corrections append, never overwrite.

contradiction.note: The exploit wrote the rule. The prettier, edited version never gets to win — the original always survives.

records.subject: Intent model fits the data
reasoning: Realms of intent (cognitive / spiritual / physical) mapped cleanly onto records being gathered.
Same model that serves personal logs also answers work questions without the lookups.
Became the STRATEGY-OPERATION-CATEGORY-STATUS intent vector.

records.subject: Domain-general intake proven (far-domain test)
reasoning: Ran the converter on a game's collecting/inventory data — a domain far from health.
It encoded and sorted without special-casing — the intake grammar generalizes.
Evidence the ledger is not a diet tracker wearing a costume.

records.subject: Encoded-truth grammar (DAQ)
reasoning: Truths get printed back encoded so human and bot anchor to the same structure.
An earlier acronym collided with a well-known one; renamed DAQ (Domain-Action-Quality), values unchanged.
Confidence carries the calibration rule: fluency is never evidence.

records.subject: Master sheets stood up
reasoning: Life, Fleet, Home-Inventory and Pantry master sheets provisioned.
Receipts feed cost records; pantry removals keep inventory true.
The skeleton every later projection reads from.

records.subject: Bi-temporal timestamps adopted
reasoning: When a thing happened vs when it was logged must not be the same field.
Late-arriving events re-project by event time, not entry time.
Became a Core Law: bi-temporal.

records.subject: The Gems — projection packages take shape

sources.name: Build log — Ain't She a Gem (01-23)

reasoning: One ledger, many cut faces — each Gem a packaged way to read it.
PRISM is the direct ancestor of tonight's flagship room.
Lenses change the cut; the stone underneath does not.

records.subject: Habits leave the to-do list, become history
reasoning: A checklist tells you what you ought to do; a log tells you what you did.
Recurring routines moved out of a task app and into tracked habits.
Care, not compliance — the bench never shames an empty evening.

records.subject: Master ledger → Lightweave, compiled into a site
reasoning: The build logs' master-ledger idea became 41 linked projection rooms.
Released only on attested completion: integrity check passed, every link resolves.
This very record is part of that run — the journey looking at itself.

records.subject: Personas as a working crew
reasoning: The Scout captures (chaos, fast), the Auditor checks (meticulous), the Steward keeps (calm).
Capture / audit / steward — the three jobs a trustworthy ledger needs.
A skin over the same canon; the crew never changes a confidence or a fact.

records.subject: Naming lineage kept on purpose
records.value: A noir naming play → the system's voice
reasoning: Two voices — one arcane, one the clerk you dictate to — both fit at different meta levels.
The naming play is part of the system's character, kept on purpose.
A thread, not a fact — tone, never truth.

records.subject: Sovereign storage — off the cloud
reasoning: The master logs must stay local, heavily encrypted — encryption tight enough to actually trust.
Tell the bot everything; keep the canon yours.
Still a direction, not a finished build — held as Watching.

records.subject: Context-limit uncertainty
records.value: How many tags can it hold?
reasoning: Recurring worry: unsure how much to sort over time, or how many tags it can process.
Volume grew fast — six hours produced a year's worth of rows if extrapolated.
Unresolved by design — surfaced as Watching rather than pretended solved.

records.subject: Voice-input friction
reasoning: Voice chat was rough — pauses to collect thoughts got cut off; background noise dropped input.
Logged honestly as a degraded channel, not hidden.
Minor, low-stakes — Watching.

records.subject: A sealed personal record
reasoning: A personal record exists in this window.
Sealed by an Iris policy at the operator's request — the projection shows that it exists, never its contents.
Governance: SENSITIVE / SELF / LONG.

records.subject: A sealed private channel
reasoning: A private channel for watching a trend with care, not judgement.
Sealed RESTRICTED — existence and intent shown; nothing about the contents leaves the instance.
Kept because trend-watching matters; sealed because it is no one else's business.

records.subject: A sealed, unresolved record
reasoning: A record whose readings conflict, kept rather than resolved.
Held as Contested — the ledger preserves the conflict instead of picking a side.
Sealed: the projection acknowledges the weight without exposing a word of it.

contradiction.note: Both are kept. The ledger does not pick the kinder version — it keeps the real one, and it keeps it private.
